anthropological definition of environment anthropological definition of environment the anthropological definition of environment refers to the multifaceted and dynamic interplay between human societies and the natural world. anthropologists consider the environment to be not just the physical or biotic surroundings, but also the cultural, social, and symbolic contexts in which humans interact with their ecosystem. this definition encompasses the ways in which human populations perceive, interpret, and influence their environment, as well as the reciprocal impact of the environment on human behavior, social organization, and cultural practices. it includes the natural resources, landscapes, and ecosystems that sustain human life, as well as the cultural meanings, values, and beliefs that people assign to these elements anthropological definition of environment anthropological definition of environment ecological anthropology attempts to understand the relative influence of environment on human societies and how it is used by different societies.
